name: Embalse de los Rodeos y Antigua estacion por la Via Verde del Noroeste — desde Molina de Segura
name_original: Embalse de los Rodeos y Antigua estación por la Vía Verde del Noroeste — desde Molina de Segura
region_intro: Touring cycling around Molina De Segura features routes through diverse landscapes, including the Segura River's agricultural environment and surrounding mountains. The region is characterized by fertile valleys, dedicated bike paths, and natural landmarks such as the Embalse de Santomera. Terrain varies from flat riverside sections to more challenging climbs in areas like the Sierra de la Pila Regional Park. Best touring cycling routes around Molina De Segura The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse de los Rodeos y Antigua estacion por la Via Verde del Noroeste — desde Molina de Segura, a 18.4 miles (29.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a greenway, passing by a reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is La Contraparada of the Segura River – Red Iron Bridge loop from Molina de Segura, a difficult 51.0 miles (82.1 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, offering panoramic views from higher terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Vía Verde del Noroeste – Red Iron Bridge loop from Molina de Segura, a 19.0 miles (30.6 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 44 minutes. Touring cycling around Molina De Segura is defined by the Segura River's agricultural plains, gentle mountain slopes, and scenic reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more demanding climbs. The routes in Molina De Segura are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Molina De Segura's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Molina De Segura?
A: There are over 40 touring cycling routes around Molina De Segura, offering diverse experiences for cyclists of all levels.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Molina De Segura?
A: Yes, Molina de Segura offers several easy routes. For instance, the "Ruta Circular Molino - Alguazas" is an easy 30-kilometer ride through an agricultural setting, perfect for those new to touring cycling. You can also find gentler sections along the Segura River.

Q: What kind of challenges can advanced touring cyclists expect in Molina De Segura?
A: Advanced cyclists seeking a challenge can find routes with significant elevation gain and demanding terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40377179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Calarria loop from Los Olivos&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 40.4 km path featuring over 630 meters of ascent, rewarding riders with superb panoramic views from higher elevations like Puerto El Coto and La Perdiz.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Molina De Segura?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38253573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde del Noroeste – Red Iron Bridge loop from Molina de Segura&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 30.6 km circular trail that takes you through varied landscapes.

Q: What are some scenic natural features I can expect to see on touring cycling routes?
A: The routes often showcase the enchanting agricultural environment of the Segura River, with green fields and Mediterranean fruit plantations. You'll also encounter natural landmarks like the Embalse de Santomera (Santomera Reservoir) and can enjoy views of gentle mountains and fertile valleys. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3152197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Contraparada of the Segura River&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural highlight.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Molina De Segura?
A: Yes, the dedicated bike paths along the Segura River are generally suitable for families, offering flat sections through parks, gardens, and picnic areas. These routes provide a safe and enjoyable experience for all ages.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Molina De Segura?
A: Molina de Segura benefits from a favorable Mediterranean climate almost year-round, making it ideal for outdoor pursuits. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for touring cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer.

Q: What kind of cultural or historical points of interest can I explore while cycling?
A: You can combine your cycling with local tourism. The "Medieval Molina Route" leads to the Castle viewpoint, offering beautiful views of the Huerta de Murcia. The Ricote Valley, accessible from the area, also combines natural beauty with opportunities for cultural stops. You might also pass by historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3833926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Real Casino of Murcia&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5456784" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Romea Theatre&lt;/a&gt; if your route takes you towards Murcia City.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about touring cycling in Molina De Segura?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 40 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the tranquil agricultural plains along the Segura River to the more challenging mountain slopes, and the well-maintained paths like those found on the Vía Verde del Noroeste.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or water bodies?
A: Yes, several routes feature water bodies. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017153173/embalse-de-los-rodeos-y-antigua-estacion-por-la-via-verde-del-noroeste-desde-molina-de-segura" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de los Rodeos y Antigua estacion por la Via Verde del Noroeste — desde Molina de Segura&lt;/a&gt; is a popular route that passes by a reservoir. The Embalse de Santomera is another frequently mentioned natural landmark that features in various cycling routes, offering picturesque views.

Q: Can I find routes that connect Molina de Segura to other towns?
A: Yes, there are routes that connect Molina de Segura to neighboring towns. An intermediate route connecting Molina de Segura to Santomera and other nearby towns provides scenic diversity and cultural points of interest, allowing you to explore beyond the immediate municipality.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I observe along the touring cycling routes?
A: For nature observation and bird watching, the Campotéjar wetlands are recommended, home to species like the endangered white-headed duck. While cycling, you might spot various birds and local fauna, especially in the greener areas along the Segura River and near natural reserves.
tips: A sample of the beautiful architecture that accompanied this line of the old Murcia-Caravaca Railway, now refurbished as a Vía Verde.
Close to the Vía Verde del Noroeste, we arrive by a comfortable path to the Mirador del Embalse de Rodeos, an exceptional place for nature lovers to take a break and/or take some pictures.

waypoints.name: View of Embalse de los Rodeos
